dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic won because Claude-branded tools got repeated, concrete credit for real engineering work—a large C++→Rust port and a custom FreeBSD Wi‑Fi driver—while the main setback was a narrower complaint about Claude app memory/prompt behavior on a reasoning test [E1][E3][E4].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic had the broadest and strongest direct signal on the front page: one high-rank thread praised Claude Code for helping ship a regression-free Rust port, and another praised Sonnet for producing a useful driver patch that likely would not have been written otherwise. That outweighed the separate criticism that Claude app memories/bio context could derail the car-wash answer [E1][E3][E4].

openai.judgementSnippet: Codex was praised alongside Claude Code for accelerating Ladybird’s C++→Rust translation under strict byte-for-byte and zero-regression checks, but OpenAI only had that single direct front-page signal [E2].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude Code and Sonnet were repeatedly credited for speeding up difficult coding tasks, from a large Rust port to a FreeBSD driver, though Claude app memory/bio behavior drew a visible complaint in the car-wash reasoning thread [E1][E3][E4].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Google’s AI answer in Search was singled out for getting the car-wash question right by focusing on the need to bring the car to the wash, but it was just one anecdotal mention rather than a broader Gemini discussion [E5].

evidence.summary: In the Ladybird thread, a top comment quoted the author saying Claude Code helped translate about 25,000 lines from C++ to Rust in two weeks with byte-for-byte identical output and zero regressions, then praised coding assistants for this kind of porting work.

The same Ladybird comment said Codex was used for the translation and adversarial review, framing the result as work that would have taken months by hand but was finished in weeks without regressions.

In the FreeBSD driver thread, a commenter said Sonnet 4.6 produced a small working patch in minutes that let them keep an old macOS build working, and another comment reinforced the value of model-generated planning/spec documents for large tasks.

In the car-wash test thread, a commenter reported Sonnet 4.5 answering incorrectly and Opus 4.6 failing when Claude app memories or biographical context were enabled, calling the behavior 'stupid' and 'sad'.
A commenter highlighted Google Search’s AI answer as correctly solving the car-wash question by explicitly reasoning that the car has to be physically present at the wash.
